basic_details.job_title: CG Vyapam Sub Auditor Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 69 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Chhattisgarh Professional Examination Board (CG Vyapam), Raipur
basic_details.department: Office of Commissioner Cooperative & Registrar, Cooperative Institutions, Chhattisgarh
basic_details.short_information: Chhattisgarh Professional Examination Board (CG Vyapam) has published the Sub Accountant (COSA26) Notification 2026 on 29 April 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill 69 Sub Auditor (Upa Ankekshak) positions. Candidates within the prescribed age limit and holding a Bachelor’s Degree from a recognized university are eligible to apply online at https://vyapamcg.cgstate.gov.in. The application process will be active from 29 April 2026 to 22 May 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

application_fee.details: Candidates applying for the COSA26 examination are required to pay the prescribed examination fee online at the time of submitting the application. The fee is non-refundable. However, as per CG Government Order RULE-8/2/2024-GAD-1 dated 21-04-2025, the examination fee will be refunded to Chhattisgarh domicile candidates who appear in the examination, directly to their bank account from which the fee was paid.

selection_process: Type of Examination OMR Based MCQ (Objective Type)
Negative Marking 1/4 mark deducted per wrong answer
Exam Centres 05 Divisional Headquarters across Chhattisgarh
The selection for Sub Accountant (COSA26) is based on a Written Examination only. The question paper will consist of 100 objective type (MCQ) questions of 1 mark each

to be answered in 2 hours (120 minutes) on OMR sheet using blue/black ball point pen. Negative marking of 1/4 mark is applicable for each wrong answer. Candidates who leave a question unattempted will be awarded zero marks for that question. Candidates must darken only one option on the OMR sheet.

syllabus.details: The detailed syllabus for the Sub Accountant (COSA26) written examination is available on the official CG Vyapam website under the “Syllabus” link as prescribed by the concerned department. | Syllabus Link: Click Here

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online only through the CG Vyapam portal at https://vyapamcg.cgstate.gov.in. No other mode of application will be accepted. The online portal is active from 29-04-2026 to 22-05-2026 (5:00 PM). First-time applicants must complete Profile Registration (Panjikaran) on the Vyapam portal - once registered, candidates need not re-enter personal information for future exams. After completing profile registration, candidates must pay the examination fee online and click the “SUBMIT” button to finalize the application.

A correction window of 3 days (23-05-2026 to 25-05-2026) will be available after the closing date. For basic information corrections, candidates must update their Profile Registration first. Admit cards will be uploaded on the Vyapam website approximately 7 days before the examination - admit cards will NOT be sent by post. Candidates must download and print their admit card from the Vyapam portal. The exam result will be published on https://vyapamcg.cgstate.gov.in. Re-evaluation/re-checking is not applicable as OMR sheets are evaluated by machine.
Items to Carry to Examination Centre:
Black or Blue Ball Point Pen
Original Photo ID Proof - Voter ID / Driving License / PAN Card / Aadhaar Card (with photograph)
important_instructions: Only bonafide residents of Chhattisgarh as per state norms are eligible to apply. Candidates must possess the prescribed minimum qualification on the closing date of application. No hard copy applications will be accepted - online submission only. Examination fee is non-refundable; however, it will be refunded to Chhattisgarh domicile candidates who appear in the exam. Candidates must reach the examination centre at least 2 hours before commencement of the exam for identity verification and frisking. Main gate of the examination centre will be closed 30 minutes before the scheduled exam time (i.e., at 9:30 AM) - latecomers will not be allowed. Mobile phones and all electronic devices, watches, purses, scarves, belts, caps etc. are strictly banned inside the examination hall. Half-sleeve light-coloured clothes must be worn; dark-coloured clothes (black, dark blue, dark green, purple, maroon, dark chocolate) are prohibited. Footwear - only slippers/sandals are permitted. Ear ornaments/jewellery of any type are prohibited. Candidates must not leave the exam hall during the first half hour and last half hour of the examination. Each objection/claim against model answers must be submitted separately for each question with a fee of Rs. 50/- per question (online only) - objections without proof will be rejected. No re-evaluation/re-checking is available as OMR sheets are evaluated by machine. For helpline: Call 0771-2972780 (working days, 10:00 AM to 5:30 PM) or mobile 8269801982 (holidays, 10:00 AM to 5:30 PM). Candidates are advised to visit https://vyapamcg.cgstate.gov.in regularly for all recruitment updates. All decisions of CG Vyapam regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding.

faq.question: Q1. What is CG Vyapam Sub Accountant (COSA26) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: CG Vyapam Sub Accountant COSA26 Recruitment 2026 is a written recruitment examination conducted by Chhattisgarh Professional Examination Board (CG Vyapam), Raipur for the post of Sub Auditor (Upa Ankekshak) in the Office of Commissioner Cooperative & Registrar, Cooperative Institutions, Chhattisgarh. The exam is notified through Notification No. Cr./Vyapam/Pariksha F‑20/2026/1775 dated 29 April 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there for CG Vyapam COSA26 Sub Auditor 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 69 vacancies have been announced for the Sub Auditor (Upa Ankekshak) post under the COSA26 recruitment. These include 29 posts for UR, 10 for OBC, 8 for SC and 22 for ST, as per the category‑wise break‑up in the departmental advertisement.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for CG Vyapam Sub Accountant COSA26 2026?
faq.answer: The official notification for COSA26 was issued on 29 April 2026. The online application window is open from 29 April 2026 to 22 May 2026 up to 5:00 PM, while the correction window is available from 23 May to 25 May 2026, and the written exam is tentatively scheduled on 5 July 2026 with admit cards likely to be released around 25 June 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility criteria (education and domicile) for CG Vyapam COSA26?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Bachelor’s Degree from a recognized university as the minimum educational qualification for the Sub Auditor post. Only eligible candidates as per the departmental advertisement and CG state norms can apply, and for fee refund benefit the candidate must be a domicile of Chhattisgarh and actually appear in the exam.

faq.question: Q5. What is the exam pattern and selection process for CG Vyapam Sub Auditor COSA26?
faq.answer: Selection is based solely on an OMR‑based written examination comprising 100 objective type questions of 1 mark each, totaling 100 marks. The exam duration is 2 hours and there is negative marking of 1/4 mark for each wrong answer, with no marks for unattempted questions.

faq.question: Q6. Is there negative marking in the CG Vyapam COSA26 examination?
faq.answer: Yes, there is negative marking in the COSA26 Sub Auditor examination. For every incorrect answer, 1/4 of the marks assigned to that question will be deducted, while no penalty applies to questions left unanswered.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application / examination fee for CG Vyapam COSA26?
faq.answer: The examination fee for COSA26 is ₹350 for General category, ₹250 for OBC and ₹200 for SC/ST/Divyang candidates, payable online at the time of application. As per Chhattisgarh Government orders, the fee amount will be refunded to Chhattisgarh domicile candidates who actually appear in the examination, directly to the bank account used for payment.

faq.question: Q8. Where will the CG Vyapam COSA26 exam be conducted?
faq.answer: The COSA26 written examination will be held at five divisional headquarters across Chhattisgarh. The coordinating centres are located at Ambikapur (Surguja), Bilaspur, Durg, Jagdalpur (Bastar) and Raipur, with specific government colleges designated as exam centres in the notification.

faq.question: Q9. How can I apply online for CG Vyapam Sub Auditor COSA26 Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online through the CG Vyapam portal vyapamcg.cgstate.gov.in by first completing profile registration and then selecting the COSA26 exam to fill the application form. They need a valid mobile number, email ID, scanned photo and signature, and must pay the examination fee online before finally submitting and printing the confirmation page.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I download the CG Vyapam COSA26 notification PDF, syllabus and admit card?
faq.answer: The official COSA26 notification PDF, detailed syllabus and other exam documents are available on the CG Vyapam website under the “Recruitment/Exam” or “Syllabus” sections. Admit cards will be released online only a few days before the exam date, and candidates must regularly visit vyapamcg.cgstate.gov.in to download the admit card and check updates, as no hard copy will be sent by post.
